label: Liquid API
title: Liquid API
lead: Register liquid definitions, fill liquid containers, and understand WaterContainerItem behavior.
bodyHtml:
What counts as a liquid?

Liquids in Casualties Unknown are not normal item IDs by themselves. A liquid is a LiquidType entry in the vanilla Liquids.Registry. A container stores one or more LiquidStack entries, where each stack points at a liquid ID and an amount in mL.

CUCoreLib works with the game's liquid system without making you write vanilla locale glue. Use LiquidRegistry.Register for liquid definitions, LiquidStack for stored contents, and direct CustomItemInfo liquid fields when you want a bottle, pouch, canteen, or other normal liquid container.

LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", new CustomLiquidInfo
{
    name = "Pineapple Juice",
    description = "You've never seen this before. A light yellow drink that smells fruity.",
    color = new Color(0.94f, 0.88f, 0.66f),
    valuePerLiter = 22f
});

CustomLiquidInfo

CustomLiquidInfo describes what a custom liquid is and what it does when used. CUCoreLib creates the vanilla LiquidType, stores it in Liquids.Registry[id], and sets vanilla localeName to the registry ID internally.

FieldTypeWhat it does
namestringDisplay name registered as other/id for locale lookup and generated locale files.
descriptionstringDescription registered as other/id + "dsc".
colorColorColor used by liquid UI, average container color, and liquid fill visuals.
valuePerLiterfloatTrade/value basis for 1000 mL of the liquid.
onDrinkLiquidType.OnDrinkCalled by WaterContainerItem.Drink(body, amount) for each drained liquid portion.
onHealthUseLiquidType.OnHealthUseCalled by limb use when allowed. Injection also uses this delegate when injectable is true.
healthUsableboolAllows ApplyToLimb to call onHealthUse.
injectableboolAllows Inject to call onHealthUse. Injection sickness can still apply even when this is false.
injectionSicknessfloatMultiplier for sickness and blood-viscosity changes during injection. Defaults to 1f.
localeFromItemboolIf true, tooltip names/descriptions come from item locale keys using the liquid ID.
qualitiesList<CraftingQuality>Liquid qualities used by recipe matching. Amounts scale by mL through GetScaledQualities.
 
LiquidRegistry.Register("lidocainecream", new CustomLiquidInfo
{
    name = "Lidocaine cream",
    description = "A numbing cream meant for bare skin.",
    color = new Color(0.92f, 0.91f, 0.84f),
    valuePerLiter = 90f,
    healthUsable = true,
    injectable = false,
    onHealthUse = (ml, limb) =>
    {
        if (limb.skinHealth > 20f)
        {
            // it does nothing because the expie is covered in fur
            return;
        }

        float dose = ml * 0.01f;
        limb.pain -= dose * 6f;
    }
});

LiquidStack

A container does not store one big mixed object, rather a list of stacks. Liquid use is used proportionally from every stack, so a mixed syringe with 50 mL water and 100 mL morphine drains both at a (1:2 ratio) when injecting.

Field / constructorTypeWhat it does
new LiquidStack(id, amount)LiquidStackCreates a stored liquid entry with an ID and amount in mL.
liquidIdstringMust match a key in Liquids.Registry, such as water or morphine. Or your newly created liquid ID!
amountfloatAmount in mL. Vanilla removes tiny remaining stacks below about 0.5f mL after drains.
 
DefaultContents =
{
    new LiquidStack("estrogen", 100f)
}

Liquid containers

The runtime component is WaterContainerItem. It owns the liquid stack list, tracks capacity and fill amount, adds or drains liquids, and calls liquid effects when the player drinks, applies, or injects contents.

CustomItemInfo inherits the vanilla LiquidItemInfo fields, so normal liquid containers use capacity, defaultContents, and autoFill directly. SyringeProperties is only for syringe minigame/injection behavior.

MemberTypeWhat it means
capacityfloatMaximum total mL for a custom liquid container item.
defaultContentsList<LiquidStack>Initial liquid stacks placed into freshly spawned custom liquid containers.
autoFillboolWhether the loose item can fill from world liquid tiles through vanilla behavior.
stackList<LiquidStack>Runtime contents stored on the spawned WaterContainerItem.
CapacityfloatRuntime maximum total mL, read from item stats.
CurrentTotalfloatTotal mL across all liquid stacks.
SpaceLeftfloatRemaining mL capacity: Capacity - CurrentTotal, clamped at zero.
AddLiquid(id, amount)floatAdds as much liquid as fits and returns the amount actually added.
Drain(...)voidRemoves calculated liquid amounts and updates item condition to match fill percentage.
Drink(body, amount)voidDrains proportionally and calls each liquid's onDrink.
ApplyToLimb(limb, amount)voidDrains proportionally and calls onHealthUse only for liquids marked healthUsable.
Inject(limb, amount)voidDrains proportionally, applies injection sickness, then calls onHealthUse for liquids marked injectable.

CUCoreLib works with the game's liquid system without making you write vanilla locale glue. Use LiquidRegistry.Register for liquid definitions, LiquidStack for stored contents, and direct CustomItemInfo liquid fields when you want a bottle, pouch, canteen, or other normal liquid container. LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", new CustomLiquidInfo { name = "Pineapple Juice", description = "You've never seen this before. Amounts scale by mL through GetScaledQualities .
